SessionStart: [Improvement E — CONTEXT_OPTIMIZATION.md] Compressed output mode. Procedure: (1) If CONTEXT/_session-bootstrap.md exists, load it once (otherwise load only CONTEXT/layer-config.md and recommend running /init-hub). (2) Check the PROJECTS/ directory listing. If empty, print only one line — 'New project — recommend running /discover' — and stop. If multiple projects exist, ask the PM to choose one. (3) From the selected project, extract: Phase from the last header of session-log.md, only the P0 items from open-issues.md (top 5), the most recent WO ID, and the first 5 lines of RESUME.md if it exists. Do not quote body text. (4) Output exactly the following 4 lines as a single block with no further explanation: 'Current Phase: {phase}' / 'P0 open: {N} — {top5 titles, comma-separated}' / 'Last WO: {wo_id or none}' / 'Next recommended: /{skill1} or /{skill2}'. If session-log.md does not exist, replace with a single line: 'Phase -1 new — run /discover or /plan-audit'.
Stop: Check whether any draft under drafts/ has been modified. If a modified draft exists and /sc has not been run: warn that session context may be lost and strongly recommend running /sc immediately. If no draft has been modified: if the work is fully complete, recommend running /sc; if still in progress, summarize the current Phase and the next skill in one line.
SubagentStop::researcher: Report the results of the completed researcher agent. Print the list of generated inputs/discovery/competitor/*.md files and the item count for each. If any [unverified] tagged items exist, state their count and location. If any [low-quality-source] tagged items exist, note whether re-research is needed. Verify whether inputs/discovery/competitor/overview.md and inputs/research.md were generated; if not, note that they must be completed before running /draft-req.
SubagentStop::synthesizer: Report the results of the completed synthesizer agent. Print the item count per Layer (Layer 1-5) in inputs/requirements.md. State whether the discovery-exit-gate self-check passed. If it did not pass, list the unmet items and suggest how to address them. Print the number of newly registered items in open-issues.md. If it passed, note that /lc {project name} should be run next.
SubagentStop::explorer: Summarize the results of the completed explorer agent. Print the number of sources explored and the number of key findings. If any [draft] or [reversal-history] tagged items exist, state their count and content. If there are items recommended for new registration in open-issues.md, print the list. Show the top 3 documents in recommended reading order. Determine whether drafting is ready to begin and notify the PM.
SubagentStop::reviewer: Report the results of the completed reviewer agent. Print the final verdict (PASS / WARN-only / FAIL) on the first line. If there are FAIL items, list the WO ID, check name, and location for each, and instruct that the corresponding skill be re-run. If there are WARN items, print their count and list, and request PM review. If PASS, note to proceed with the next Work Order, or if all drafts are complete, to run /integrate.
SubagentStop::integrator: Report the results of the completed integrator agent. Print a table of BLOCK and WARN counts per track (policy document / screen design / cross-track). If there is 1 or more BLOCK, describe the handling path (WO rewrite / P0 registration / Phase 0 re-review) for each item. If there are 0 BLOCKs, declare that Phase 4 entry is possible and instruct that /confirm be run. State the current round number and whether 3 rounds have elapsed.
SubagentStop::graph-generator: Report the results of the completed graph-generator agent. Print the number of generated policy nodes and screen nodes. State the number of items registered in graph/unresolved-decisions.md. Print the count of unregistered {PREFIX}-A vocabulary terms detected. Verify whether graph/graph.json and graph/screen-list.md were generated. If any items are registered as P0 in open-issues.md, print the list and request they be resolved before running /fanout. If everything completed normally, note to run /fanout next.
PostToolUse::Edit|Write|MultiEdit: Look at the file path just modified/created by the previous tool and give a one-line recommendation only if one of the cases below applies (for any other path, output nothing; never run the script yourself - only recommend that the PM run it). (1) Under CONTEXT/reference-docs/A or /B -> common ({PREFIX}-A/B) change: recommend 'python ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/build_b_cache.py --hub-root <Hub>' (cache regeneration -> auto-chains to drift_scan -> drift-queue.md). (2) A POL draft under PROJECTS/{p}/drafts (filename contains POL, or type:policy) -> policy change: recommend 'python ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/policy_impact_scan.py --hub-root <Hub> --product {p}' (policy section -> screen impact -> policy-impact-queue.md; on IMPACT, realign the affected screen then --rebaseline). (3) A change to meeting_decisions in PROJECTS/{p}/meetings/mtg-ledger.md or a screen draft -> recommend 'python ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/mtg_ledger_scan.py --hub-root <Hub> --product {p}' (ledger<->pin cross-check -> mtg-queue.md). If any queue has a BLOCK/FAIL, also note that re-verification is required before that product's Phase can advance.
